title: calciphilopteris papuana
intro: Calciphilopteris papuana, also known as the Papuan fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to New Guinea and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
